title: profile.yaml — the person (brain↔hands seam)
description: Produced/edited by the brain via `wsx profile set`; consumed by wsx. Stored as YAML in context/profile.yaml within the constrained subset wsxlib/yamlio.py supports (nested maps, scalars, inline flow lists).

use_context.description: What this workspace is FOR. Determined early; prioritizes everything downstream — which hubs are primary, walled-vs-blended separation, tone. 'mixed' = both, kept in their walls.

expertise.description: Per-DOMAIN skill level — NOT a single global rating. The same person is often a hobbyist in one area and a staff-level expert in another; each generated skill is written at the altitude for ITS domain. Keyed by domain slug.

seniority.description: e.g. 'staff', 'principal', 'lead' — when the domain is professional and it matters

years.description: time in the domain, when relevant

note.description: any nuance about their standing in this domain

surfaces.description: Which AI tools to emit for. 'primary' is the recommended/default surface.

contexts.description: Walled by default: work/professional/personal kept separate; personal is private.

personal_local_only.description: walled personal context: context/personal.md is gitignored, excluded from every emitted adapter, and never read unless explicitly asked. IMPLEMENTED.

encrypt.description: NOT IMPLEMENTED — declares an intent only. There is no vault-level encryption in wsx today; gitignoring a file is NOT encryption. Setting this true does not protect anything. `wsx verify` warns if it is true so nobody relies on a guarantee that does not exist. For real at-rest protection use full-disk encryption (FileVault/BitLocker) or an encrypted volume.

imports.description: existing repos / skill libraries / docs to learn from
